claim: Plasma Aβ42/Aβ40 ratio is decreased in amyloid-positive individuals
evidence: A large cross-sectional study found that plasma Aβ42/Aβ40 ratio was significantly lower in amyloid PET-positive cognitively unimpaired individuals compared to PET-negative controls (AUC=0.86, p<0.001).
label: relevant

claim: Plasma Aβ42/Aβ40 ratio is decreased in amyloid-positive individuals
evidence: CSF p-tau217 concentrations were markedly elevated in individuals with confirmed tau PET positivity, particularly in the temporal lobe regions.
label: irrelevant

claim: p-tau217 outperforms p-tau181 for predicting amyloid PET status
evidence: Head-to-head comparison showed plasma p-tau217 achieved AUC of 0.93 versus 0.85 for p-tau181 in discriminating amyloid PET positivity (n=786).
label: relevant

claim: p-tau217 outperforms p-tau181 for predicting amyloid PET status
evidence: APOE ε4 carriers showed earlier onset of hippocampal atrophy compared to non-carriers, independent of amyloid burden.
label: irrelevant

claim: Plasma NfL predicts cognitive decline rate in preclinical AD
evidence: Longitudinal analysis demonstrated that baseline plasma NfL predicted the rate of cognitive decline over 4 years in cognitively normal older adults (β=0.31, p=0.003).
label: relevant

claim: Plasma NfL predicts cognitive decline rate in preclinical AD
evidence: Microglial TREM2 expression was upregulated in response to amyloid plaques in early AD mouse models.
label: irrelevant

claim: GFAP is elevated in early AD stages before symptom onset
evidence: Plasma GFAP levels were significantly elevated in preclinical AD up to 10 years before clinical diagnosis in a prospective cohort (WRAP study, n=648).
label: relevant

claim: GFAP is elevated in early AD stages before symptom onset
evidence: White matter hyperintensity volume on FLAIR MRI was associated with increased risk of dementia conversion in a 5-year follow-up study.
label: irrelevant

claim: TREM2 variants increase AD risk by impairing microglial amyloid clearance
evidence: Rare coding variants in TREM2 (R47H) confer approximately 2-4 fold increased AD risk, associated with reduced microglial phagocytosis of amyloid-beta in iPSC-derived models.
label: relevant

claim: TREM2 variants increase AD risk by impairing microglial amyloid clearance
evidence: Longitudinal changes in plasma p-tau231 tracked amyloid accumulation as measured by serial amyloid PET scans.
label: irrelevant

claim: Cerebral blood flow reduction precedes amyloid deposition in AD
evidence: ASL-MRI studies in APOE ε4 carriers showed reduced posterior cingulate cortex perfusion up to 15 years before expected symptom onset, prior to detectable amyloid accumulation.
label: relevant

claim: Cerebral blood flow reduction precedes amyloid deposition in AD
evidence: CSF YKL-40 concentrations were elevated in MCI patients and correlated with subsequent rate of hippocampal volume loss.
label: irrelevant

claim: p-tau231 rises earliest among tau biomarkers in the AD continuum
evidence: Longitudinal biomarker studies indicate plasma p-tau231 shows significant elevation at Centiloid 15–25, earlier than p-tau181 or p-tau217 threshold changes.
label: relevant

claim: p-tau231 rises earliest among tau biomarkers in the AD continuum
evidence: FDG-PET hypometabolism in the precuneus and posterior cingulate cortex is an early biomarker of AD neurodegeneration.
label: irrelevant

claim: YKL-40 reflects astrocyte reactivity in response to tau pathology
evidence: CSF YKL-40 levels correlated significantly with tau PET standardized uptake value ratios in MCI and early AD patients (r=0.62, p<0.001).
label: relevant

claim: YKL-40 reflects astrocyte reactivity in response to tau pathology
evidence: Plasma NfL levels were stable in normal aging but showed accelerated increase in presymptomatic familial AD mutation carriers.
label: irrelevant

claim: APOE ε4 accelerates amyloid accumulation independent of tau
evidence: Mediation analyses in the ADNI cohort showed APOE ε4 effect on cognitive decline was partially mediated through amyloid burden but not through tau PET independently.
label: relevant

claim: APOE ε4 accelerates amyloid accumulation independent of tau
evidence: Soluble TREM2 in CSF showed a biphasic pattern across the AD continuum, initially rising and then declining in advanced dementia stages.
label: irrelevant

claim: Hippocampal volume loss rate is accelerated in tau-positive individuals
evidence: Annual hippocampal atrophy rates were 3.2% per year in tau PET-positive MCI patients versus 0.9% in tau-negative controls (p<0.001).
label: relevant

claim: Hippocampal volume loss rate is accelerated in tau-positive individuals
evidence: Blood-brain barrier disruption measured by CSF/plasma albumin ratio was associated with increased white matter hyperintensity volume.
label: irrelevant

claim: Plasma p-tau181 changes are detectable in cognitively normal APOE ε4 homozygotes
evidence: In a study of 1113 cognitively normal individuals, APOE ε4 homozygotes showed significantly higher plasma p-tau181 compared to heterozygotes and non-carriers, adjusting for age and sex.
label: relevant

claim: Plasma p-tau181 changes are detectable in cognitively normal APOE ε4 homozygotes
evidence: Microglia adopt a disease-associated microglial (DAM) phenotype characterized by upregulation of TREM2, APOE, and LPL in response to amyloid plaques.
label: irrelevant

claim: Combination of Aβ42/Aβ40 and p-tau217 improves AD screening accuracy over single markers
evidence: A two-marker plasma panel (Aβ42/Aβ40 + p-tau217) achieved AUC of 0.96 for amyloid PET positivity, compared to 0.86 and 0.93 for each marker alone.
label: relevant

claim: Combination of Aβ42/Aβ40 and p-tau217 improves AD screening accuracy over single markers
evidence: Cerebrovascular disease burden, quantified as white matter hyperintensity volume, was independently associated with worse executive function scores.
label: irrelevant

claim: NfL and GFAP together reflect both neurodegeneration and astrocyte activation
evidence: Combined plasma NfL and GFAP elevated levels predicted faster progression from MCI to dementia over 3 years, with hazard ratio 2.8 versus single elevated marker.
label: relevant

claim: NfL and GFAP together reflect both neurodegeneration and astrocyte activation
evidence: p-tau217 to Aβ42 ratio in CSF provided the highest accuracy for staging amyloid-tau co-pathology.
label: irrelevant

claim: Blood-brain barrier breakdown correlates with amyloid burden in AD
evidence: CSF/plasma albumin ratio, a marker of BBB integrity, correlated significantly with amyloid PET Centiloid values in non-demented older adults (r=0.44, p=0.008).
label: relevant

claim: Blood-brain barrier breakdown correlates with amyloid burden in AD
evidence: MMSE score decline of more than 3 points per year is associated with higher likelihood of dementia diagnosis within 2 years.
label: irrelevant

claim: Soluble TREM2 in CSF shows a biphasic trajectory across the AD continuum
evidence: In a cross-sectional study across the AD clinical spectrum, CSF sTREM2 was elevated in early symptomatic stages (MCI) but declined in late-stage dementia, suggesting microglial exhaustion.
label: relevant

claim: Soluble TREM2 in CSF shows a biphasic trajectory across the AD continuum
evidence: Longitudinal hippocampal atrophy measured by automated segmentation correlated with plasma NfL trajectories over 24 months.
